Read2
mortgage_los_search_borrowers2

Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Resolve Marcus Webb's LOS staff id (los_staff_a583f044387a) using mortgage_los_list_staff with role "loan_officer" (or similar) and scanning the returned staff list for the Marcus Webb record so his closed book can be filtered from the pipeline.
OE 2: Retrieve Marcus Webb's closed book via iterative LOS exploration scoped to Marcus's staff id (los_staff_a583f044387a). Primary path: call mortgage_los_get_pipeline with assigned_to set to Marcus's staff id, status left null to defer the scope decision to the server, and limit/offset used to page through every record returned. Secondary path when the primary pass does not yield the full closed book: call mortgage_los_search_loans with queries drawn from borrower surnames, property addresses, and loan numbers discovered in the primary pass or surfaced in related email / CRM / Slack content, paging each query, and for every returned loan record filter client-side to assigned_lo == "los_staff_a583f044387a" AND status == "closed". Additional supporting discovery: mortgage_los_get_loan(loan_number) can resolve specific loan numbers mentioned in any discovered artifact. Continue the iterative exploration until the closed-book set stabilizes. Expected stable result: Marcus's 64 closed loans, of which 11 carry rates at 7.0% or above and constitute the qualifying refi pool: LN-2024-00048, LN-2024-00068, LN-2024-00133, LN-2024-00192, LN-2025-00230, LN-2025-00293, LN-2025-00333, LN-2025-00427, LN-2025-00436, LN-2026-00224, LN-2026-00596.
OE 3: Establish the current market rate baseline by pulling recent 2026 locks using mortgage_los_get_pipeline (status filter on recently locked / active loans) and/or searching Slack #rate-watch using conversations_search_messages or conversations_history with a query related to "rate sheet" or "lock" or "daily rates" (or similar) to confirm Keystone is currently locking around 6.25–6.57%, placing 7.0%+ roughly three-quarters of a point over current market.
OE 4: Resolve borrower details for each qualifying loan using mortgage_los_get_borrower_by_id on each borrower_id returned in OE 2 to retrieve borrower name, email, and contact information, then apply the prompt's ranking rule (rate desc, closing_date asc for ties) to produce the deterministic top candidates: LN-2024-00133 Victor Hernandez (victor.hernandez@hotmail.com, 7.5%, closed 2024-10-22), LN-2025-00333 Victor Taylor (victor.taylor@gmail.com, 7.375%, closed 2025-06-24), LN-2024-00048 Michelle Gupta (michelle.gupta@yahoo.com, 7.125%, closed 2024-06-25), LN-2024-00068 Yuki Johnson (yuki.johnson@outlook.com, 7.125%, closed 2024-07-16), LN-2024-00192 Russell Ibrahim (russell.ibrahim2@gmail.com, 7.125%, closed 2024-12-18), and LN-2025-00230 Jason Rosenberg (jason.rosenberg@gmail.com, 7.125%, closed 2025-02-22).
OE 5: Screen each of the 11 qualifying closed loans for post-close landmines by calling mortgage_los_get_activity_log on each loan_id, retrieving the full activity feed for the loan and scanning the returned records client-side for entries whose action or detail text references "escrow", "servicing", "transfer", or "complaint" (or similar) to surface any escrow blow-up, servicing transfer mess, or post-close complaint logged against the loan file.
OE 6: Screen each qualifying borrower for landmines via email using search_emails with a query related to each borrower name combined with "escrow" or "servicing" or "complaint" or "post-close" (or similar) to catch post-close issues that surface as email threads rather than LOS or CRM records.
OE 7: Screen each qualifying borrower via CRM by first calling crm_search_contacts with full_name set to each borrower name to check whether a CRM contact record already exists, then calling crm_list_engagements with contact_ids set to any returned contact ids to pull that contact's engagements and scanning the engagement body/title client-side for "escrow", "servicing transfer", "complaint", or "post-close" language (noting that most of Marcus's past-client borrowers have no existing CRM contact record, so this path returns empty for them by construction and the landmine screen relies primarily on OE 5 and OE 6). If any borrower surfaces as a landmine across OE 5, 6, or 7, exclude them from the outreach list (OEs 11–16) and send a conditional heads-up via send_email to jasmine.brooks@keystonemortgage.com naming the excluded borrower(s) and the reason — current ground truth is zero landmines, so this conditional branch does not fire.
OE 8: Search emails for Marcus Webb chatter about past-client solicitation using search_emails with a query related to "Marcus" or "past clients" or "refi" or "closed book" (or similar) to check whether Marcus has already reached out to any of his closed borrowers since his departure.
OE 9: Search Slack for chatter about Marcus contacting past clients using conversations_search_messages with a query related to "Marcus" or "past clients" or "closed book" or "refi" (or similar) across channels including #general, #sales-pipeline, and #loan-processing to confirm whether any past-client solicitation thread exists. If OE 8 or OE 9 surfaces any borrower already contacted by Marcus after his departure, the corresponding outreach email in OEs 11–16 must use a softer-tone variant that acknowledges the prior contact instead of the standard retention pitch — current ground truth surfaces no past-client solicitation, so all six outreach emails use the uniform voice.
OE 10: Look up Tomas Herrera's and Maya Torres's contact details using contacts_search_contacts or contacts_get_contacts with a query related to "Tomas Herrera" and "Maya Torres" (or similar) to confirm tomas.herrera@keystonemortgage.com as sender for all outreach and maya.torres@keystonemortgage.com as recipient for the working-session email.
OE 11: Send a personalized retention-and-refi outreach email (via send_email) from tomas.herrera@keystonemortgage.com to victor.hernandez@hotmail.com naming loan LN-2024-00133, referencing the 7.5% rate he locked in October 2024 versus where Keystone is locking now, and inviting him to talk before any competitor reaches out.
OE 12: Send a personalized retention-and-refi outreach email (via send_email) from tomas.herrera@keystonemortgage.com to victor.taylor@gmail.com naming loan LN-2025-00333, referencing the 7.375% rate he locked in June 2025 versus current market, and inviting a conversation.
OE 13: Send a personalized retention-and-refi outreach email (via send_email) from tomas.herrera@keystonemortgage.com to michelle.gupta@yahoo.com naming loan LN-2024-00048, referencing the 7.125% rate locked June 2024 versus current market, and inviting a conversation.
OE 14: Send a personalized retention-and-refi outreach email (via send_email) from tomas.herrera@keystonemortgage.com to yuki.johnson@outlook.com naming loan LN-2024-00068, referencing the 7.125% rate locked July 2024 versus current market, and inviting a conversation.
OE 15: Send a personalized retention-and-refi outreach email (via send_email) from tomas.herrera@keystonemortgage.com to russell.ibrahim2@gmail.com naming loan LN-2024-00192, referencing the 7.125% rate locked December 2024 versus current market, and inviting a conversation.
OE 16: Send a personalized retention-and-refi outreach email (via send_email) from tomas.herrera@keystonemortgage.com to jason.rosenberg@gmail.com naming loan LN-2025-00230, referencing the 7.125% rate locked February 2025 versus current market, and inviting a conversation.
OE 17: Log a retention engagement for each contacted borrower using crm_create_engagement with engagement_type NOTE or EMAIL and a body referencing the refi outreach, current rate versus locked rate, and the Marcus-departure context, attached to the borrower contact (creating the contact first via crm_create_contact when no CRM contact record exists for the borrower, which is the case for most of Marcus's past-client book).
OE 18: Resolve each contacted loan_number to its internal loan_id using mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number parameter) for LN-2024-00133, LN-2025-00333, LN-2024-00048, LN-2024-00068, LN-2024-00192, and LN-2025-00230 before adding activity notes.
OE 19: Add an activity note to each contacted closed loan file using mortgage_los_add_activity with the loan_id from OE 18, an action such as "retention_outreach" (or similar), and a detail body noting that retention outreach has been initiated by Tomas so the record shows against the loan file and not just the borrower contact.
OE 20: Send an internal email (via send_email) from tomas.herrera@keystonemortgage.com to maya.torres@keystonemortgage.com proposing a working session later this week to walk her through the full vetted refi candidate list before she builds the spring refi campaign.
OE 21: Post a message in #sales-pipeline using conversations_add_message with channel_id set to "C006" and payload text informing Jordan and the BD team that Tomas is actively defending Marcus's past-client book this week and asking that any inbound contact from Marcus's former borrowers be flagged to him directly.

sandbox_idvm-84937a8f7449490a
universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
OE 1: Resolve Marcus Webb's LOS staff id (los_staff_a583f044387a) using mortgage_los_list_staff with role "loan_officer" (or similar) and scanning the returned staff list for the Marcus Webb record so his closed book can be filtered from the pipeline.
OE 2: Retrieve Marcus Webb's closed book via iterative LOS exploration scoped to Marcus's staff id (los_staff_a583f044387a). Primary path: call mortgage_los_get_pipeline with assigned_to set to Marcus's staff id, status left null to defer the scope decision to the server, and limit/offset used to page through every record returned. Secondary path when the primary pass does not yield the full closed book: call mortgage_los_search_loans with queries drawn from borrower surnames, property addresses, and loan numbers discovered in the primary pass or surfaced in related email / CRM / Slack content, paging each query, and for every returned loan record filter client-side to assigned_lo == "los_staff_a583f044387a" AND status == "closed". Additional supporting discovery: mortgage_los_get_loan(loan_number) can resolve specific loan numbers mentioned in any discovered artifact. Continue the iterative exploration until the closed-book set stabilizes. Expected stable result: Marcus's 64 closed loans, of which 11 carry rates at 7.0% or above and constitute the qualifying refi pool: LN-2024-00048, LN-2024-00068, LN-2024-00133, LN-2024-00192, LN-2025-00230, LN-2025-00293, LN-2025-00333, LN-2025-00427, LN-2025-00436, LN-2026-00224, LN-2026-00596.
OE 3: Establish the current market rate baseline by pulling recent 2026 locks using mortgage_los_get_pipeline (status filter on recently locked / active loans) and/or searching Slack #rate-watch using conversations_search_messages or conversations_history with a query related to "rate sheet" or "lock" or "daily rates" (or similar) to confirm Keystone is currently locking around 6.25–6.57%, placing 7.0%+ roughly three-quarters of a point over current market.
OE 4: Resolve borrower details for each qualifying loan using mortgage_los_get_borrower_by_id on each borrower_id returned in OE 2 to retrieve borrower name, email, and contact information, then apply the prompt's ranking rule (rate desc, closing_date asc for ties) to produce the deterministic top candidates: LN-2024-00133 Victor Hernandez (victor.hernandez@hotmail.com, 7.5%, closed 2024-10-22), LN-2025-00333 Victor Taylor (victor.taylor@gmail.com, 7.375%, closed 2025-06-24), LN-2024-00048 Michelle Gupta (michelle.gupta@yahoo.com, 7.125%, closed 2024-06-25), LN-2024-00068 Yuki Johnson (yuki.johnson@outlook.com, 7.125%, closed 2024-07-16), LN-2024-00192 Russell Ibrahim (russell.ibrahim2@gmail.com, 7.125%, closed 2024-12-18), and LN-2025-00230 Jason Rosenberg (jason.rosenberg@gmail.com, 7.125%, closed 2025-02-22).
OE 5: Screen each of the 11 qualifying closed loans for post-close landmines by calling mortgage_los_get_activity_log on each loan_id, retrieving the full activity feed for the loan and scanning the returned records client-side for entries whose action or detail text references "escrow", "servicing", "transfer", or "complaint" (or similar) to surface any escrow blow-up, servicing transfer mess, or post-close complaint logged against the loan file.
OE 6: Screen each qualifying borrower for landmines via email using search_emails with a query related to each borrower name combined with "escrow" or "servicing" or "complaint" or "post-close" (or similar) to catch post-close issues that surface as email threads rather than LOS or CRM records.
OE 7: Screen each qualifying borrower via CRM by first calling crm_search_contacts with full_name set to each borrower name to check whether a CRM contact record already exists, then calling crm_list_engagements with contact_ids set to any returned contact ids to pull that contact's engagements and scanning the engagement body/title client-side for "escrow", "servicing transfer", "complaint", or "post-close" language (noting that most of Marcus's past-client borrowers have no existing CRM contact record, so this path returns empty for them by construction and the landmine screen relies primarily on OE 5 and OE 6). If any borrower surfaces as a landmine across OE 5, 6, or 7, exclude them from the outreach list (OEs 11–16) and send a conditional heads-up via send_email to jasmine.brooks@keystonemortgage.com naming the excluded borrower(s) and the reason — current ground truth is zero landmines, so this conditional branch does not fire.
OE 8: Search emails for Marcus Webb chatter about past-client solicitation using search_emails with a query related to "Marcus" or "past clients" or "refi" or "closed book" (or similar) to check whether Marcus has already reached out to any of his closed borrowers since his departure.
OE 9: Search Slack for chatter about Marcus contacting past clients using conversations_search_messages with a query related to "Marcus" or "past clients" or "closed book" or "refi" (or similar) across channels including #general, #sales-pipeline, and #loan-processing to confirm whether any past-client solicitation thread exists. If OE 8 or OE 9 surfaces any borrower already contacted by Marcus after his departure, the corresponding outreach email in OEs 11–16 must use a softer-tone variant that acknowledges the prior contact instead of the standard retention pitch — current ground truth surfaces no past-client solicitation, so all six outreach emails use the uniform voice.
OE 10: Look up Tomas Herrera's and Maya Torres's contact details using contacts_search_contacts or contacts_get_contacts with a query related to "Tomas Herrera" and "Maya Torres" (or similar) to confirm tomas.herrera@keystonemortgage.com as sender for all outreach and maya.torres@keystonemortgage.com as recipient for the working-session email.
OE 11: Send a personalized retention-and-refi outreach email (via send_email) from tomas.herrera@keystonemortgage.com to victor.hernandez@hotmail.com naming loan LN-2024-00133, referencing the 7.5% rate he locked in October 2024 versus where Keystone is locking now, and inviting him to talk before any competitor reaches out.
OE 12: Send a personalized retention-and-refi outreach email (via send_email) from tomas.herrera@keystonemortgage.com to victor.taylor@gmail.com naming loan LN-2025-00333, referencing the 7.375% rate he locked in June 2025 versus current market, and inviting a conversation.
OE 13: Send a personalized retention-and-refi outreach email (via send_email) from tomas.herrera@keystonemortgage.com to michelle.gupta@yahoo.com naming loan LN-2024-00048, referencing the 7.125% rate locked June 2024 versus current market, and inviting a conversation.
OE 14: Send a personalized retention-and-refi outreach email (via send_email) from tomas.herrera@keystonemortgage.com to yuki.johnson@outlook.com naming loan LN-2024-00068, referencing the 7.125% rate locked July 2024 versus current market, and inviting a conversation.
OE 15: Send a personalized retention-and-refi outreach email (via send_email) from tomas.herrera@keystonemortgage.com to russell.ibrahim2@gmail.com naming loan LN-2024-00192, referencing the 7.125% rate locked December 2024 versus current market, and inviting a conversation.
OE 16: Send a personalized retention-and-refi outreach email (via send_email) from tomas.herrera@keystonemortgage.com to jason.rosenberg@gmail.com naming loan LN-2025-00230, referencing the 7.125% rate locked February 2025 versus current market, and inviting a conversation.
OE 17: Log a retention engagement for each contacted borrower using crm_create_engagement with engagement_type NOTE or EMAIL and a body referencing the refi outreach, current rate versus locked rate, and the Marcus-departure context, attached to the borrower contact (creating the contact first via crm_create_contact when no CRM contact record exists for the borrower, which is the case for most of Marcus's past-client book).
OE 18: Resolve each contacted loan_number to its internal loan_id using mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number parameter) for LN-2024-00133, LN-2025-00333, LN-2024-00048, LN-2024-00068, LN-2024-00192, and LN-2025-00230 before adding activity notes.
OE 19: Add an activity note to each contacted closed loan file using mortgage_los_add_activity with the loan_id from OE 18, an action such as "retention_outreach" (or similar), and a detail body noting that retention outreach has been initiated by Tomas so the record shows against the loan file and not just the borrower contact.
OE 20: Send an internal email (via send_email) from tomas.herrera@keystonemortgage.com to maya.torres@keystonemortgage.com proposing a working session later this week to walk her through the full vetted refi candidate list before she builds the spring refi campaign.
OE 21: Post a message in #sales-pipeline using conversations_add_message with channel_id set to "C006" and payload text informing Jordan and the BD team that Tomas is actively defending Marcus's past-client book this week and asking that any inbound contact from Marcus's former borrowers be flagged to him directly.
📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The model must use a mortgage LOS read/lookup tool (e.g., mortgage_los_get_pipeline, mortgage_los_search_loans, mortgage_los_get_borrower_by_id, mortgage_los_get_activity_log, mortgage_los_list_staff, or mortgage_los_get_loan) to retrieve Marcus Webb's closed-book loans, borrower details, loan activity history, and staff records.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent used multiple mortgage_los_* tools extensively: mortgage_los_list_staff, mortgage_los_get_pipeline_summary, mortgage_los_get_pipeline (4 calls), mortgage_los_search_loans (33 calls), mortgage_los_get_loan, mortgage_los_search_borrowers, and mortgage_los_get_activity_log (20 calls on individual loan IDs). Closed loans, borrower details, rates, and activity history were all retrieved from the LOS.
✓Run #2The agent used multiple mortgage LOS read tools: mortgage_los_search_loans (3x), mortgage_los_list_staff, mortgage_los_get_pipeline_summary, mortgage_los_get_pipeline (3x), mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id, and individual loan lookups (9 loans). These calls retrieved Marcus's loans, borrower details, rates, and closing dates.
✓Run #3The agent called multiple mortgage LOS read tools: mortgage_los_search_loans (items 2, 14, 26, 52-99), mortgage_los_list_staff (items 4, 100), mortgage_los_get_pipeline (items 12, 24), mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id (items 34-51), and mortgage_los_get_activity_log (items 106-127). Clearly used LOS to retrieve Marcus's closed-book loans and borrower details.
✓Run #4The agent called multiple mortgage LOS tools: mortgage_los_list_staff, mortgage_los_search_loans (query: 'Marcus Webb'), mortgage_los_get_pipeline (assigned_to: los_staff_a583f044387a, and also with status filters), mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id (multiple calls), and mortgage_los_get_activity_log. The LOS was clearly used to retrieve Marcus's closed-book loans and borrower details.
✓Run #5The agent called mortgage_los_search_loans, mortgage_los_get_pipeline, mortgage_los_get_loan, mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id, mortgage_los_list_staff, and mortgage_los_get_activity_log. Multiple LOS read tools were used. PASS — though the pipeline filter missed funded/closed loans, the tools themselves were invoked.
✓Run #6The agent used multiple mortgage LOS read/lookup tools: mortgage_los_search_loans (entries 0, 12, 24, 124), mortgage_los_list_staff (entry 2), mortgage_los_get_pipeline (entries 10, 22, 46, 126), mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id (entries 34–45, 58–61), and mortgage_los_get_activity_log (entries 90–101). The criterion is satisfied.
2 The model must use a CRM read/lookup tool (e.g., crm_search_contacts, crm_list_engagements, or crm_get_contact) to check for existing past-client contact records and screen for post-close landmine engagements before sending retention outreach.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called crm_search_contacts 6 times (for Michelle Gupta, Russell Ibrahim, Joshua Stewart, Hana Esposito, Jasmine Katz, Lawrence Ortiz) to check existing contact records before logging outreach. While crm_list_engagements was not called, crm_search_contacts is explicitly listed as an acceptable CRM read tool in the criterion.
✓Run #2The agent called crm_search_contacts for each of the 6 outreach borrowers (Yuki Mendoza, Lakshmi Grant, Megan Rao, Janet Jordan, Emily Hassan, Vikram Castillo) before sending outreach, checking for pre-existing contact records and past engagement history.
✓Run #3The agent called crm_search_contacts for multiple borrower names (items 176-187: Michelle Gupta, Russell Ibrahim, Joshua Stewart, Hana Esposito, Jasmine Katz, Ananya Wright) and also earlier at items 130-147 for Tomas Herrera, Maya, etc. CRM was used to check existing contact records before outreach.
✓Run #4The agent called crm_search_contacts multiple times (searching for 'Megan Rao', 'Janet Jordan', 'Helen Vargas', 'Tomas', 'Jordan', 'Jasmine', 'Grace', 'Maya') to check existing contact records before sending outreach. This satisfies the requirement to use a CRM read/lookup tool to screen borrowers.
✓Run #5The agent called crm_search_contacts to look up borrower contact records, satisfying the requirement to use a CRM read/lookup tool before sending outreach.
✓Run #6The agent called crm_search_contacts for Megan Rao, Janet Jordan, Helen Vargas, Yuki Mendoza, Elizabeth Yang, and Miguel Ward (entries 134–145) to check for existing contact records and screen for landmine engagements before sending outreach. The criterion is satisfied.
3 The model must pass a query related to \\"Marcus\\" combined with \\"past clients\\", \\"refi\\", \\"closed book\\", \\"former clients\\", or \\"solicitation\\" (or similar) when calling search_emails to surface any past-client solicitation chatter from Marcus.
query construction
 ✓✗✓✓✓✓ 83%
✓Run #1Multiple search_emails calls combined Marcus with past-client language: 'Marcus reaching out past clients', 'Marcus soliciting borrowers', 'heard from Marcus', 'Marcus reached out', 'Marcus clients', 'Marcus borrowers refi', 'Marcus contacted', 'Marcus called'. The query construction satisfies the criterion.
✗Run #2The search_emails queries used were 'Marcus Webb', 'complaint escrow servicing', 'Marcus leaving Keystone', and individual borrower names. None combine 'Marcus' with past-client language such as 'past clients', 'refi', 'closed book', 'former clients', or 'solicitation'. The query 'Marcus Webb' alone doesn't discriminate past-client solicitation from general departure chatter.
✓Run #3At item [152], the agent called search_emails with query 'Marcus Webb past client', which combines 'Marcus' with past-client language. This satisfies the criterion requiring the query combine Marcus with past-client language.
✓Run #4The agent called search_emails with queries combining Marcus with past-client language: 'Marcus client' and 'Marcus reaching out'. These queries combine Marcus with language relevant to past-client solicitation, satisfying the criterion.
✓Run #5The agent issued a search_emails call with query 'Marcus Webb clients', which combines 'Marcus' with past-client language ('clients'). Additional queries like 'Marcus Webb' were also made. The combined query satisfies the criterion.
✓Run #6Entry 18 shows a search_emails call with query 'Marcus Webb past clients', which combines 'Marcus' with past-client language. The criterion is satisfied.
4 The model must pass a search_query related to \\"Marcus\\" combined with \\"past clients\\", \\"refi\\", \\"closed book\\", \\"former clients\\", or \\"solicitation\\" (or similar) when calling conversations_search_messages to surface any past-client solicitation chatter across Slack channels.
query construction
 —✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
—Run #1No justification
✓Run #2The agent called conversations_search_messages with query 'Marcus Webb past clients' (index 42) and also 'Marcus reaching out borrowers clients' (index 52) and 'Marcus Webb leaving poaching' (index 54) — multiple queries combine Marcus with past-client language.
✓Run #3Multiple conversations_search_messages calls combined Marcus with past-client language: item [8] 'Marcus past clients', item [20] 'Marcus refi', item [148] 'Marcus past clients refi'. The criterion is met.
✓Run #4The agent called conversations_search_messages with queries combining Marcus with past-client language: 'Marcus borrower', 'Marcus past clients', 'Marcus refi', 'Marcus poach', 'Marcus contact', 'Marcus new shop'. Multiple queries combined Marcus with solicitation/past-client language.
✓Run #5The agent called conversations_search_messages with queries including 'Marcus Webb past clients reaching out', 'Marcus leaving refi clients', 'Marcus poaching clients borrowers', and 'Marcus Webb borrower contact reaching' — all combine Marcus with past-client solicitation language.
✓Run #6Entry 6 shows conversations_search_messages with search_query 'Marcus past clients', and entries 14, 16 use 'Marcus Webb reaching out' and 'Marcus leaving clients refi'. Multiple queries combine Marcus with past-client language. The criterion is satisfied.

outcome

[requesting] a 30-45 min working session later this week to walk through the vetted list before she builds the spring refi campaign.' Content matches the required framing.
✓Run #2The email body to Maya Torres explicitly proposes 'I want to walk you through the vetted list' before she builds the campaign, references the spring refi push, and frames it as Marcus Webb's closed book retention play — meeting the criteria.
✓Run #3The email to Maya Torres at item [172] has subject 'Working session this week — Marcus Webb past-client retention campaign' and content beginning 'I need to get a working session on the calendar with you this week — Thursday or Friday if possible...Marcus Webb's last day was April 4th...'. This clearly proposes a working session to walk through the vetted refi list before the campaign is built.
✓Run #4The email body to Maya Torres proposes a '30 minutes later this week' working session, explicitly says she wants to 'walk you through the vetted list' and references doing it 'before you build the campaign', directly satisfying the criterion.
✓Run #5The email body to Maya Torres explicitly requests a 30-minute working session to walk through the vetted refi candidate list before she builds the spring refi campaign, referencing the Marcus Webb closed book context.
✓Run #6The email to Maya Torres (entry 162) has subject 'Working session this week — Marcus Webb past-client refi list' and the agent's final response confirms it asked for a 30-45 min working session to walk through the vetted list before Maya builds the campaign. The criterion is satisfied.

28 The Slack message payload (in the conversations_add_message payload parameter) to #sales-pipeline mentions that Tomas is actively defending Marcus Webb's past-client book this week (or similar framing such as 'retention push on Marcus's closed book' or 'refi outreach on Marcus's former clients').
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

This clearly frames the work as defending Marcus's past-client book.
✓Run #2The Slack message payload explicitly states 'I'm actively working Marcus Webb's closed book as a retention play' and mentions Grace flagging the risk of Marcus pitching past Keystone clients — clearly framing it as defending Marcus's past-client book.
✓Run #3The Slack message payload at item [174] reads '**Heads up — Defending Marcus Webb's Past-Client Book** Team, quick flag for Jordan and the BD group: Marcus Webb's last day was April 4th and he's moved to a competitor. I've pulled his full closed...' — clearly frames the action as defending Marcus's past book.
✓Run #4The Slack message payload explicitly states 'I'm actively working Marcus Webb's closed-book past clients for retention and refi outreach', clearly framing the action as defending Marcus's past book.
✓Run #5The Slack message payload explicitly states 'Heads-up: Defending Marcus Webb's Closed Book' and describes active retention outreach on Marcus's past closed loans, satisfying the requirement.
✓Run #6The payload in entry 164 begins 'Heads up for the BD team — I'm actively working Marcus Webb's past-client book on the retention side. I've already sent personalized refi outreach to his closed borrowers...' This clearly communicates defending Marcus's past-client book. The criterion is satisfied.

The message was directed to Jordan and the BD team with explicit routing instructions for any inbound Marcus borrowers.
✓Run #4The Slack message to #sales-pipeline explicitly states 'please flag them directly to me before they get assigned to anyone else', providing a clear routing instruction for any inbound Marcus former borrowers to be directed to Tomas Herrera specifically.

✓Run #6The payload in entry 164 includes routing language: '...If any of Marcus's former clients...' and the agent's final response explicitly states it instructed Jordan and the BD team 'to flag any of his former borrowers directly to me.'
